description: Commits made in detached HEAD are abandoned when the next checkout moves HEAD, since no branch ever referenced them.
body: Failure mode: after checking out a tag or specific commit, work is committed while HEAD is detached, then a checkout or switch moves HEAD away, leaving those commits unreachable and slated for garbage collection. Detection signals: git status warned about detached HEAD; recent commits are absent from every branch's log; only git reflog still lists them. Do not do substantial work in detached HEAD without creating a branch. Safer fallback: run git switch -c <branch> before leaving the detached state, and if work was already lost, recover the hash from the reflog and branch from it.
